title: handle_like and handle_announce create duplicate notifications
description: In inbox.rs, handle_like (line 1264) and handle_announce (line 1326) use plain INSERT INTO notifications without OR IGNORE. The notifications table has a unique dedup index (idx_notifications_dedup) on (account_id, kind, from_account_id, from_remote_account_id, post_id, remote_post_id). When a remote server resends the same Like or Announce (retries, software bugs), the INSERT violates the UNIQUE constraint, causing an unhandled sqlx error that propagates to the HTTP response as a 500 Internal Server Error. The remote server then interprets this as a transient failure and retries, creating a retry loop. The fix is to use INSERT OR IGNORE (matching the DM path at line 977 which correctly checks before inserting). This differs from handle_follow which uses INSERT OR IGNORE.
